response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and backend engineer with strong event-driven architecture and polyglot experience, but the role requires deep, sustained C++ expertise for industrial automation systems — a domain where the candidate has only a brief 6-month engagement at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. Critical role requirements — IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle, industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0), multi-threaded C++ systems design, and industrial IoT development — are entirely absent from the resume. The candidate's career trajectory is firmly web and cloud-oriented, making them a poor structural fit for a mid-senior C++ systems role in industrial automation manufacturing.
strengths: Brief but contextually relevant C++ and C work at National Instruments on engineering measurement software (LabWindows/CVI) provides some industrial software familiarity
Demonstrated event-driven and high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers) maps loosely to message-broker architectures
Consistent agile practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring via dojos and workshops — align with the role's behavioral competency expectations
concerns: C++ experience amounts to roughly 6 months of professional work plus a thesis project; nowhere near the depth required to architect and lead high-performance industrial C++ platform development
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge: no IEC 62443, no SDL implementation, no secure embedded or protocol-level development in any listed role

No exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0) or IoT application development; primary domain expertise is web, SaaS, and cloud, not automation or embedded systems
